titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Erica Van Horn Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday July 23, 2019 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 84 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Erica Van Horn on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Work Files, 1982-2011. II. Documentation, 1970s-2010s. III. Contributions, 1978-2010s. IV. Unrealized projects, 1980s-2000s. V. Objects, 1970s-2010s. VI. Notebooks, 1974-2012. VII. Correspondence, 1970s-2010s. VIII. Ephemera, 1980s-2010s. IX. Electronic media, 2000s.

bioghist.head: Erica Van Horn
bioghist.p: Erica Van Horn is an American artist, writer, editor, printer, and bookmaker. She was born in Concord, New Hampshire and has a Master of Fine Arts in Printmaking from the School of the Art Institute of Chicago. Her work now mostly issues through Coracle, a small publishing press based in Ireland that she directs with Simon Cutts, a writer, artist, and editor with whom Van Horn has had a long collaborative relationship.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Erica Van Horn Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Erica Van Horn papers, which span from 1970s-2011, contains artworks, mock-ups, correspondence, notebooks, objects, clippings, photographs, ephemera, electronic media, and other papers related to Van Horn's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Erica Van Horn Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains project files, clippings, photographs, artwork, ephemera, and other papers related to Van Horn's work.

did.unittitle: Speculation Laville Aux Dames, Un Oeuf et une Chaise, and Creation Perfection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes alternate photocopy and concertina versions of works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Howard Hibbert, photocopies of drawings, and draft of text from Contemporary Japanese edited by Howard Hibber.

did.unittitle: 25 Avenue Anatole, France and the Neighborhood

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a sewn photocopy of book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original artworks.

did.unittitle: Doomed to a Life of Compulsive Meaninglessness

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original artwork, photocopies of original monoprints, and Japanese stitchwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original artworks.

did.unittitle: Scraps of an Aborted Collaboration, He Wears Green Shoes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters from Galerie Cassandra, Drobak, and Norway.

did.unittitle: Files and papers for exhibition in Sodertalje, Sweden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents of folder are sewn together into a book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photocopies of notes taken during "Art Creating Society" conference.

did.unittitle: The Importance of Being Ian Hamilton Finlay

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes, writings, and revisions.

did.unittitle: The Garlic Book, at the Derwent Gallery Derby, UK

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contiains materials from unique book made for Tamsin Clark, whose parents Tom and Laurie Clark run the Cairn Gallery.

did.unittitle: Form Card Number 21 Marmalade, with Harry Gilonis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photocopies, notes, monoprints, cards, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two test pieces for The Project Room.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains lists and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photo of Roman Cafe, cards printed on train station machines, lists, notes, photographs, mockups, photocopies, and versions of "The Addresses of Nora Barnacle and James Joyce," "Trieste 1905-1915," and "Italian Lesson no.7" (glass mockup).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts, notes, and a dummy draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes small edition made for a daughter of friends, photocopied texts, drawings, a dummy draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, plans, and cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a copy of the work, along with related texts from 2009.

did.unittitle: Living Locally number 10: Against Mice Burn Sage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes and a card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes, monoprints, templates, and other papers related to Sprigs of Rosemary , Rosemary Plaque , and Living Locally number 14: Rosemary .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains dummy drafts, unfinalized artwork, notes, and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains samples and tests, writings, and planning notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original drawings and monoprints, letterpress cards from Punch Press, poem manuscript by Tom Meyers, tracings, proofs, and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains test and practice pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains dummies, rejected versions, drawings, notes, and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original monoprint drawing, photocopies, rough dummies, notes, test versions, and planning notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a dummy, notes, and a poem manuscript by Tom Meyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscript versions and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains dummies, cloth samples, templates, printings, and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and laserprints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains tranparencies, letters, announcement reviews, and diagrams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, floor plan, and planning notes.

scopecontent.p: Contains diagrams, lasercopies, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drawings, correspondence, ammouncement card, and planning notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains installation instructions, wall text, and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes and a rough dummy copy.

scopecontent.p: Contains catagory lists and box notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains slides, notes, photographs, and other materials related to Van Horn's artistic work.

did.unittitle: I've Been Making Books Since the Day President Kennedy Was Shot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes transparencies, slides, photographs, photocopies, contact sheets, manuscripts, notes, and a small book made as an apology for teasing Erica and making her rip up the original book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes contact sheets with images of Paris houseboat, Chateau de LesVault, McDowell Colony, Cite des Artes, Two Saints in Search of a Plague, painted silk fans, Rudiger Schottle show in Florence, workshop in Docking, Jonathan Williams, Tom Meyer, and Harry Gilonis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, laserprints, and endpaper details.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, slides, and negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, slides, and negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, negatives, and related papers for projects including St. Cloud Mural, Ants in Space installation at McDowell Colony, Bysantine Border at Chateau de Lesvault, Sainted and Demons at Womens studio workshop.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides for Your United States, Meses, Dorchest, Moi Meme, The New Man, and 15 Men in a Book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides for Notes in a Cheesebox, Bertha, Desmond, flag collages with Richard Doherty photos, Cat Souveniers, Birds of America, Tent, Speculations, Favorite Clothes Who Died in France, Choisissez-Vous, Paris, and Souvenier No. 17 among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides of Odyssey: Paris, Lundi: I Restrict Myself to 8 Favorite Things a Day, Cinja, Abe Sada (pink covered version/first state), and Luzille (book), among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides of Eins, Zwei, Drei; La Maison de Ma Souer A Brulee, La Maison de Michelle Fekir, I am trying to Exorcise the Demons... No.3, Portrait of the Artist as a Bigger Person, and The Vitry Illuminations, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides of Seven Lady Saints (grey), Early American Portraits, Luzille (The painting, Chicago), John Napomuck and Saint Agatha, Retro-View, Narrative Sequence I (With Herbert Humdrich), Narrative Sequence II (with H.H.), The New Woman, Napolean's Dog, Boy with Gloves, Man with Striped Tie, and I am Trying to Exorcise the Demons... No.6, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides of Franklin Furnace installation, Demon Taking Hold No. 1, Demon Taking Hold No. 2, Demon Taking Hold No. 3, The Armchair in Which Saint Bernadette was Sitting When She Died, Jeanne D'Arc, and Woman Beating Evil, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides of Saint Sebastian monoprints (large versions), painted postcards, Demon Tryptych, Feet Paintings, and The Seven Virtues (book), among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beinecke Library, newspaper piece.

did.unittitle: Poster for Cutts and Van Horn--Equivalent Spaces, Japan
Chronological cuttings, cards for exhibitions, and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes book and price lists, reading lists, notebooks, and project files.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Simon Cutts and others
Photographs of J.W. and Tom, at the Clarks' party in Manchester

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes a notebook, tracings, lay-out details, descriptive texts, photographs, transparencies, laserprints, first pin-up calandar recipient list, and other work papers.

did.unittitle: Photographs of book fair and Didier's pizza party
Chateau de Les Vault event photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains slides of Monster Prints, Truck Book, House Book, prints and house paintings, Brentwood words, prints from California, more California, and calligraphy, among others.

#3 Iluminated books, Cite Artes Paris, Seven Virtues
#2 St. Cloud Boston, Ants in Space instalation and others.
#5 Odyssey, Jewels Je Ne Parle Pas
#7 Luzille, Luzille Book, LaVille Aux Dames, Fav. Clothes

Various slides, Chuck's space, Pam, Onlay, Norway
Livre Pour La Mur, Fish dinner, Aeroplane, Croation perfection.
Moyen Age 3 + IV, Gothic 1+2 Vitry ILL.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
